---
title: "Tutorials"
sidebar: tutorials
toc: false
---

These tutorials follow a single synthetic dataset — 1,000 financial customers
tracked daily across 15 lifecycle states — from raw data through clustering
and visualization. Each tutorial is self-contained; jump to any section.

| Tutorial | What you will learn |
|---|---|
| [Data Structures](data-structures.qmd) | `Alphabet`, `SequencePool`, sequence types, `Trajectory` |
| [Descriptive Statistics](statistics.qmd) | Entropy, turbulence, normative indicators, transition rates |
| [Distance Computation](distance.qmd) | OM, Hamming, LCS, DTW — when to use each metric |
| [Clustering](clustering.qmd) | PAM, CLARA, hierarchical; quality metrics and discrepancy |
| [Visualization](visualization.qmd) | All nine built-in plot types and plotnine composition |

::: {.callout-note}
Tutorials assume yasqat is installed. See [Installation](../getting-started/installation.qmd)
if you are just getting started.
:::

## Dataset

All tutorials use a synthetic dataset generated by `yasqat.synthetic`:

```python
from yasqat.synthetic.financial import generate_customer_sequences

df, alpha = generate_customer_sequences(
    n_customers=1000,
    n_days=365,
    seed=42,
)
```

This produces 1,000 customer trajectories across 15 lifecycle states
(Active, Dormant, Churned, etc.) sampled from a Markov chain calibrated
to approximate real financial customer behaviour.
